name: Desde Madrigalejo, Navalvillar de Pela y Orellana de la Sierra — circular en BTT
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Madrigalejo offer diverse terrain, transitioning from wild mountain ranges to flatter, irrigated plains. The region is characterized by a mix of dehesas, plains, and river environments along the Pizarroso and Ruecas rivers. Extensive irrigated lands with fruit trees and rice fields provide unique scenic routes. This varied landscape allows for a range of cycling experiences, from challenging mountain trails to more relaxed rides across agricultural areas. Best mountain bike trails around Madrigalejo The most popular mountain bike trail is Desde Madrigalejo, Navalvillar de Pela y Orellana de la Sierra — circular en BTT, a 32.3 miles (51.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This route explores the varied landscapes surrounding Madrigalejo, including agricultural areas and natural environments. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Orellana Main Canal Aqueduct – Orellana de la Sierra loop from Madrigalejo, a moderate 37.5 miles (60.4 km) path. This route offers views of the Orellana Main Canal Aqueduct and the natural beauty of the Sierra region. Local mountain bikers also love the Birdwatching viewpoint – Madrigalejo Market loop from Madrigalejo, a 26.4 miles (42.4 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Madrigalejo is defined by dehesas, irrigated agricultural lands, and river environments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through plains to more moderate trails with varied elevation. The routes in Madrigalejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Madrigalejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Madrigalejo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Madrigalejo offer a diverse landscape. You'll find a mix of dehesas (wooded pastures), plains, and river environments along the Pizarroso and Ruecas rivers. Extensive irrigated lands with fruit trees and rice fields also provide unique scenic routes, transitioning from wild mountain ranges to flatter, agricultural areas.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Madrigalejo?
A: Yes, Madrigalejo offers trails suitable for various ability levels. There are 5 easy routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An example of an easy route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madrigalejo Market – Secondary Irrigation Canal loop from Madrigalejo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 33.4 km with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Madrigalejo?
A: Madrigalejo is characterized by its varied ecosystems. Spring is particularly beautiful when fruit trees in the irrigated lands burst into bloom. Summer offers picturesque views of rice fields filled with water. Generally, spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures for cycling, while winter can also be suitable depending on weather conditions.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike routes in Madrigalejo?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, the region's 5 easy routes are generally suitable for families. These routes often traverse flatter, irrigated plains and agricultural areas, providing a more relaxed cycling experience. Always check the route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Madrigalejo?
A: Many outdoor trails in natural areas allow dogs, especially if kept on a leash. However, specific regulations can vary. It's always best to check local signage or official park guidelines for any restrictions on bringing dogs, particularly in protected natural environments or agricultural zones you might encounter.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Madrigalejo?
A: Yes, Madrigalejo is a town that promotes outdoor activities, including cycling. You should find suitable parking facilities within the town, especially near parks or starting points for routes. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for recommended parking spots.

Q: Can I reach the mountain bike trails in Madrigalejo using public transport?
A: Madrigalejo is accessible by public transport, though options for transporting bicycles might be limited depending on the service. It's recommended to check with local bus or train operators regarding their policies for carrying bikes. Alternatively, driving to Madrigalejo and parking is a common option for cyclists.

Q: Are there any interesting natural features or viewpoints to see while mountain biking?
A: Absolutely. The trails often pass through dehesas, along river banks, and past irrigated agricultural lands. You can also visit specific points of interest like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic sections.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available around Madrigalejo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Madrigalejo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana Main Canal Aqueduct – Orellana de la Sierra loop from Madrigalejo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 60.4 km path offering views of the aqueduct and the Sierra region.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Madrigalejo?
A: The mountain bike routes in Madrigalejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, which include agricultural areas, dehesas, and river environments, offering a varied and engaging cycling experience.

Q: Are there bike rental or repair shops in Madrigalejo?
A: Madrigalejo's local tourism initiatives promote cycling, suggesting a supportive environment for bikers. While specific rental or repair shops are not detailed in the guide data, it's advisable to check local directories or inquire upon arrival for services that cater to cyclists.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike route I can find in Madrigalejo?
A: One of the longer routes available is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellana Main Canal Aqueduct – Orellana de la Sierra loop from Madrigalejo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 60.4 km. This moderate trail offers a substantial ride through the region's natural beauty.

Q: Can I combine mountain biking with exploring nearby natural areas?
A: Absolutely. Madrigalejo serves as a central point for exploring some of Extremadura's best natural areas. It's situated within a short distance of the Villuercas Geopark, as well as the Siberia and Serena regions, which are renowned for their natural attractions and likely offer a wider array of mountain biking trails for more rugged terrain.
tips: Orellana de la Sierra is a town located in the old middle of the Sierra de Pela, covered by its northern slope but elevated above the rest of the terrain. It is a good town to see the whole of the Orellana Reservoir.

Navalvillar de Pela is the gateway to the region of La Siberia on the route that comes from Villanueva de la Serena. In ancient times it belonged to Trujillo, in Cáceres, and its inhabitants are known as 'peleños'.
Market Hall. Building constructed in 1959, recently renovated; unique architecture with brick facade and gabled roof.
